sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Stephanie Morales, a 36-year-old female, presents with ankle pain and a history of diabetes and high blood pressure.
She reports that she slipped on ice and twisted her left ankle two days ago, resulting in pain, swelling, and difficulty walking.
She has tried ibuprofen, ice, and elevation for pain and swelling, with minimal relief.
The pain is localized to the outside of her ankle, with no pain in her calf, lower leg, or knee.
Past medical history: Stephanie has a history of diabetes and high blood pressure.
She is taking metformin and has a hemoglobin A1c of 6.8.
She is due for an eye exam in the next couple of weeks.
Review of systems: None mentioned.
Current medications:
Metformin
Norvasc
Objective:
Vital signs: BP 120/70, no other vital signs mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's neck exam is fine, lungs are clear, heart exam is normal with a 2/6 systolic ejection murmur (unchanged from before).
The belly exam is good.
The ankle exam reveals tenderness over the lateral malleolus and the fifth metatarsal, with swelling, redness, and pain with valgus stressing of the ankle.
The patient has normal sensation and pulses in the affected area.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I suspect an ankle sprain, but would like to rule out a fracture, which is why I am ordering an x-ray.
Problem list: Ankle sprain; Suspected fracture; Diabetes; High blood pressure
Plan:
I prescribed naprosyn 500mg twice a day for pain control.
I will give the patient an air splint and crutches to avoid weight-bearing until the x-ray results are available.
I will follow up with the patient to discuss the results of the x-ray and adjust the plan accordingly.
I will also order another hemoglobin A1c to check baseline labs.
I will refill the patient's Norvasc as needed.
The patient should continue with her current diabetes management plan and keep her eye appointment scheduled.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me after the x-ray results are available, which will be in the next couple of days.
